Pricing & Support

SWE-Squad is open source at heart. The complete platform is available under the MIT license at no cost, with optional enterprise support for organizations that need guaranteed response times, deployment assistance, and custom integrations.

Choose Your Plan

Every feature, every integration, every provider — the open source version is the complete product. Enterprise support adds dedicated assistance and custom development on top of the same fully-featured platform.

Open Source

Community

Free forever

The complete SWE-Squad platform — not a limited version. Every feature is included with no gates, no premium tiers, and no surprises.

  • Full MIT-licensed codebase
  • All features included — no feature gating
  • Community support via GitHub Discussions
  • Self-hosted deployment
  • Access to all providers and integrations
  • Regular updates and patches
Get Started on GitHub
Enterprise

Enterprise Support

Contact us

Everything in the open source release, plus dedicated support and custom services for organizations with production-critical requirements.

  • Everything in Open Source, plus:
  • Priority support with guaranteed response times
  • Deployment assistance and architecture review
  • Custom provider development
  • SLA-backed uptime guarantees
  • Dedicated Slack channel
Contact Sales

No Feature Gating

Open source IS the complete product

SWE-Squad does not hold back features behind a paywall. The open source release is the same codebase used by the core team — every provider, every integration, every capability. There are no premium tiers for core functionality, no artificial limits on incidents or teams, and no plans to change that.

Enterprise support exists to help organizations that need dedicated assistance, not to unlock features. If you can self-host and self-support, you have the complete product at zero cost.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is the open source version really the complete product?

Yes. The MIT-licensed repository contains every feature, provider integration, and capability that SWE-Squad offers. There is no separate "enterprise edition" with additional features. Enterprise support adds human assistance, not software capabilities.

How do I get started with self-hosted deployment?

Clone the repository, run npm install, and start the development server with npm run dev. The documentation covers full deployment instructions, configuration options, and environment setup for production use.

What does enterprise support include?

Enterprise support provides priority response times for issues, hands-on deployment assistance, architecture reviews for your specific infrastructure, custom provider development for proprietary systems, SLA-backed uptime guarantees, and a dedicated Slack channel for real-time communication with the core team.

Ready to get started?

Explore the documentation to set up SWE-Squad for your team, or contact our team to discuss enterprise support options for your organization.
